title: Resolve central contracts reproducibly in isolated worktrees
goal: Make heim-pc and other consumers validate Metarepo contracts from an explicit, identity-bound source without depending on an accidental sibling checkout path or environment propagation through a particular execution wrapper.

acceptance.assertion: Consumers resolve contracts from an explicit CLI argument, immutable checked-out source or manifest-bound cache with documented precedence; ambient sibling paths are never silently authoritative.
Validation receipts include contract repository identity, commit, dirty state, schema path and schema SHA-256 together with consumer head and validated artifact hashes.
The same resolver works in a clean local worktree, GitHub Actions checkout, detached source archive and approved offline cache without changing semantic results.
Missing, dirty-unapproved, wrong-repository or incompatible contract sources return typed errors and cannot be mistaken for successful validation.
Grabowski synchronous and durable execution routes preserve or explicitly reject required environment and source arguments; the validator does not rely on hidden shell initialization.
Tests cover sibling checkout, explicit path, immutable archive, missing environment, wrong commit, dirty schema, CI layout and concurrent contract movement.

metadata.observation: The local validate_contracts path expected a sibling Metarepo checkout or environment override; Grabowski's synchronous route did not preserve the attempted environment form, while CI succeeded with an explicit _metarepo checkout.
